title: Regex for Text Cleaning
concept: Regex helps strip HTML tags, remove URLs, and normalize whitespace in raw datasets before modeling.

exercises.prompt: Why do we need to clean raw text datasets?

options.text: To remove code, web links, and messy spacing before training AI models.
To make the font size of the text larger.
To translate the text into another language.

exercises.explain: Text cleaning removes non-human language elements (like code tags and web links) so that AI models only process actual text.

cards.title: Messy Text, Clean AI
cards.body: Before an AI can read a book, an article, or a tweet, it needs clean text. But raw data scraped from the web is messy—filled with ugly HTML tags, random links, and chaotic spacing. How do we sweep it all away in a fraction of a second? With the magic of Regex!

cards.title: What is Text Cleaning?
cards.body:
Regex (Regular Expressions) for text cleaning uses smart search patterns to find and delete unwanted clutter. It lets you instantly strip out HTML tags (like `
`), remove web links (URLs), and collapse messy spacing into clean, single spaces. This prepares your text so AI models can understand it without getting distracted by computer code.

cards.title: The Veggie Wash
cards.body:
Think of text cleaning like washing fresh vegetables before cooking. The raw text from the internet is covered in "dirt" like messy HTML tags (

,

) and extra spaces. Using regular expressions is like putting the vegetables in a colander and spraying them with water—it washes away all the unwanted bits instantly, leaving you with clean, ready-to-use ingredients for your machine learning models.

cards.title: Cleaning in Action
cards.body: Imagine we have this raw text: `"Go to website.com now!"` Using a cleaning regex in Python: 1. We search for HTML tags `<[^>]+>` and replace them with nothing. 2. We search for multiple spaces `\s+` and replace them with a single space. Our clean output: `"Go to website.com now!"`

cards.title: The Copy-Paste Trap
cards.body: Some people think they need to write millions of individual rules to delete every possible HTML tag or extra space. This is a myth! A single, short regex pattern can find and remove every single HTML tag in an entire library of books in just milliseconds.

cards.title: Before vs. After Cleaning
cards.body: See how much easier it is to read text once regex washes away the web code and fixes the spacing!

exercises.prompt: What does the regex pattern `<[^>]+>` help remove from scraped web text?
exercises.explain: HTML tags are enclosed in angle brackets (< and >). The pattern <[^>]+> matches '<' followed by one or more characters that are not '>', and finally '>'.

exercises.prompt: True or False: Regex can be used to scan a document and automatically delete all web links (URLs).
exercises.explain: Regex is excellent at finding patterns like 'http' followed by text to match and remove URLs.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the blank to describe the goal of removing duplicate spacing:
exercises.sentence: Using regex to replace multiple spaces helps normalize the text by removing extra _____.
exercises.explain: Replacing sequence patterns like \s+ with a single space helps clean up extra spaces and makes the text uniform.

exercises.prompt: You scraped 10,000 blog posts, but they are full of messy HTML tags. What is the best way to handle this?

options.text: Write a regex script to automatically find and strip all HTML tags and normalize spacing in a millisecond.
Leave the raw HTML tags in, because AI models learn better when the text is cluttered and messy.
Hire someone to manually read every webpage and press backspace on the HTML tags.

exercises.explain: Option A is correct because regex is incredibly fast and standard for stripping formatting, while Option B is factually incorrect (AI prefers clean text) and Option C is an inefficient manual approach.

exercises.prompt: You are building a spam filter and get raw email data full of HTML tags like `<div>` and weird double-spacing. How should you clean this text using regex?

options.text: First strip all HTML tags using a pattern like `<[^>]+>`, then replace multiple spaces with a single space.
Delete all punctuation first, which automatically removes HTML tags and web links.
Manually type out and replace every single HTML tag name one by one in Python.
Train a neural network to read the text and guess where the tags are.

exercises.explain: To clean HTML tags and clean up whitespace, you first use a pattern like `<[^>]+>` to find and replace everything inside angle brackets with an empty string, and then normalize the leftover spacing.
